heading: Rollback Strategy: Always Have One
items: Deploy behind feature flags. If a new feature breaks, flip the flag off without redeploying. LaunchDarkly and Unleash are popular tools, or build a simple flags table in your database.
Use blue-green deployments. Run two identical environments (blue and green). Deploy to the inactive one, test it, then switch traffic. If something breaks, switch back in seconds.

Make database migrations reversible. Every migration should have a down() method. Never drop columns in the same deploy that stops using them -- do it in a follow-up deploy after the code change is stable.
Keep the previous build artifact. If your CI builds a Docker image tagged v1.2.3, do not delete v1.2.2 until v1.2.3 is confirmed stable. You can roll back by redeploying the old tag.
Monitor after every deploy. Watch error rates, response times, and user-facing metrics for 30 minutes after deploying. Automated alerts should fire if error rates spike above baseline.
